subject: Geology
title: Carbon and Climate
description: A 1-minute de-risking session on Carbon and Climate.

hook.text: Burning fossil fuels releases carbon that was locked underground for millions of years. The atmosphere is changing.

buildup.text: Fossil fuels return ancient carbon to the air as CO2. This enhances the greenhouse effect and warms the climate.

discovery.text: Ice cores show CO2 levels are higher now than at any point in 800,000 years. The rise matches fossil fuel use.

twist.text: Carbon capture aims to re-bury CO2 underground. Geology provides both the problem's source and a possible fix.

climax.text: The carbon cycle connects geology, energy, and climate. What we extract from the Earth changes its atmosphere.

punchline.text: Burning the past changes the future.

quiz.question: What do ice cores reveal about CO2 levels?
options: CO2 has been constant for millions of years
Current CO2 levels are higher than any in 800,000 years
CO2 levels only change during volcanic eruptions
